datasets.name: temporal_trends_contingency_table.csv
datasets.description: Dataset contains temporal trends in the introduction pathways of non-native flora (plants) in the region of Catalonia.

columns.introduction.period: This column represents different time periods, related to when non-native plant species were introduced into the region.
columns.pathway: This column represents different modes or routes through which non-native plant species were introduced, such as "AgriForest" (agriculture and forestry - plants introduced for cultivation to provide food or timber), "Gardening" (plants introduced for cultivation as ornamentals or for medicinal use), and "Unintentional" (plants introduced accidentally with the sowing of contaminated seed lots, global trade and tourism).
columns.n: This column represents the frequency or count of non-native plant species introductions for each combination of introduction period and pathway.

datasets.name: phylogenetic_tree.txt
datasets.description: This file contains a phylogenetic tree in Newick format, illustrating the evolutionary relationships among diverse plant species. It features species from multiple families, with branch lengths denoting genetic distances or evolutionary times.

datasets.name: invasion_success_pathways.csv
datasets.description: This dataset provides comprehensive information on various non-native plant species that have invaded plots in the Barcelona province. The dataset includes detailed information on the ecological and biological characteristics of each species, the extent of their invasion, and the pathways through which they were introduced.

columns.Scientific_name: The full scientific name of the non-native plant species, including the author citation.
columns.family: The botanical family to which the species belongs.
columns.number.of.invaded.plots: The number of different plots in the Barcelona province where the species has been observed to invade.
columns.area.of.occupancy.10km: The area of occupancy at a 10 km scale, indicating how widespread the species is within the studied region.
columns.habitat.range: The number of different habitat types the species can occupy.
columns.invaded.niche.breadth: A measure (index) of the ecological niche breadth of the species within the invaded region, indicating its ecological versatility.
columns.mrt: The mean residence time of the species, indicating how long the species has been present in the new region (in years).
columns.native.niche.breadth: A measure (index) of the ecological niche breadth of the species in its native range.
columns.intro.pathway: The pathway through which the species was introduced to the new region.
columns.growth.form: The growth form of the plant species, describing its general structural category.
columns.dispersal: The primary method of dispersal for the species.
columns.vegetative: Indicates whether the species can reproduce vegetatively (Yes or No).
columns.height: The typical height of the species in meters.
columns.genus: The genus to which the species belongs.
columns.species: The specific epithet of the species.
columns.genus.relative: Data relative to the genus, if applicable (specific data not provided, thus marked as NA).
columns.duplicated: Indicates if the species data is duplicated within the dataset (TRUE or FALSE).
columns.species.relative: Data relative to the species, if applicable (specific data not provided, thus marked as NA).
columns.phylo.tip.label: The label used for the species in the phylogenetic tree included in the dataset.

datasets.name: invaded_niche_pathways.csv
datasets.description: The data for the analysis of pathway-specific differences in the invaded niche.

columns.n.gard: The count or frequency of non-native plant species introduced through the "Gardening" pathway.
columns.n.unint: The count or frequency of non-native plant species introduced through the "Unintentional" pathway
columns.n.agfo: The count or frequency of non-native plant species introduced through the "AgriForest" (Agriculture and Forestry) pathway
columns.n.total: The total count or frequency of non-native plant species across all introduction pathways.
columns.habitat: A categorical variable representing the habitat type, selected from the ten most widespread habitat types in the Barcelona province
columns.elevation: The elevation (in meters) of the sampled plot or location
columns.cropland.1956.50m: This variable represent the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 50 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1956
columns.urban.1956.50m: This variable represent the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer (within a 50-meter radius) of 50 meters around the sampled plot or location for the year 1956
columns.cropland.1993.50m: This variable represent the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 50 meters (within a 50-meter radius)  around the sampled plot or location for the year 1993
columns.urban.1993.50m: This variable represent the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 50 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1993
columns.cropland.2009.50m: This variable represent the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 50 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 2009
columns.urban.2009.50m: This variable represent the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 50 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 2009
columns.cropland.1956.500m: This variable represent the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 500 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1956
columns.urban.1956.500m: This variable represent the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 50 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1956
columns.cropland.1993.500m: This variable represent the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 500 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1993
columns.urban.1993.500m: This variable represents the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 500 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1993.
columns.cropland.2009.500m: This variable represents the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 500 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 2009.
columns.urban.2009.500m: This variable represents the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 500 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 2009.
columns.cropland.1956.1000m: This variable represents the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 1000 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1956.
columns.urban.1956.1000m: This variable represents the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 1000 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1956.
columns.cropland.1993.1000m: This variable represents the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 1000 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1993.
columns.urban.1993.1000m: This variable represents the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 1000 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 1993.
columns.cropland.2009.1000m: This variable represents the percentage of cropland in a buffer of 1000 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 2009.
columns.urban.2009.1000m: This variable represents the percentage of urban land cover in a buffer of 1000 meters (within a 50-meter radius) around the sampled plot or location for the year 2009.
columns.progressive.1956.2009.50m: This variable represents the historical landscape changes in a 50-meter buffer around the sampled plot or location between 1956 and 2009. It categorizes changes as progressive, indicating an increase in urban or cropland areas over this period.
columns.regressive.1956.2009.50m: This variable represents the historical landscape changes in a 50-meter buffer around the sampled plot or location between 1956 and 2009. It categorizes changes as regressive, indicating a decrease in urban or cropland areas over this period.
columns.no.changes.1956.2009.50m: This variable represents the historical landscape changes in a 50-meter buffer around the sampled plot or location between 1956 and 2009. It categorizes areas where there have been no significant changes in urban or cropland cover over this period.
columns.progressive.1993.2009.50m: This variable represents the historical landscape changes in a 50-meter buffer around the sampled plot or location between 1993 and 2009. It categorizes changes as progressive, indicating an increase in urban or cropland areas over this period.
columns.regressive.1993.2009.50m: This variable represents the historical landscape changes in a 50-meter buffer around the sampled plot or location between 1993 and 2009. It categorizes changes as regressive, indicating a decrease in urban or cropland areas over this period.
columns.no.changes.1993.2009.50m: This variable represents the historical landscape changes in a 50-meter buffer around the sampled plot or location between 1993 and 2009. It categorizes areas where there have been no significant changes in urban or cropland cover over this period.
columns.distance.stream: The distance (in meters) from the sampled plot or location to the nearest main stream or waterway.
columns.distance.road: The distance (in meters) from the sampled plot or location to the nearest main road.
columns.longitude: The geographic coordinates of the sampled plot or location.
columns.latitude: The geographic coordinates of the sampled plot or location.
columns.annual.temperature: The mean annual temperature of the sampled plot or location.
columns.annual.rainfall: The annual precipitation or rainfall of the sampled plot or location.
columns.annual.radiation: The mean annual solar radiation (in kJ/m^2/day) of the sampled plot or location.

queries.question: Over which time period has gardening surpassed agriculture as the main contributor to the non-native flora?
What activity has replaced agriculture as the main contributor to the non-native flora over the past millennium?
In what way has gardening impacted agriculture as a contributor to non-native flora over the past millennium?
